plainEnglish: Any content that moves, blinks, scrolls, or auto-updates—carousels, animations, news tickers, live feeds—that starts automatically, lasts more than 5 seconds, and sits alongside other content must have a control to pause, stop, or hide it. Motion is distracting or disabling for many users, so give them an off switch.
whyItMatters: Users with attention-related, cognitive, or vestibular disabilities can't focus on or read a page when auto-playing motion or auto-updating content can't be paused.
quickCheck: Look for any auto-starting motion or auto-updating content lasting over 5 seconds and confirm there's a visible pause/stop/hide control; axe flags <blink> and <marquee>.
commonMistakes: Auto-rotating carousels or sliders with no pause button.
Using <marquee> or <blink>, or CSS animations that loop indefinitely with no control (axe blink/marquee).
Auto-refreshing feeds or live tickers with no way to pause or slow the updates.
Background video or animation that loops with no stop control.

codeExamples.label: Carousel with a pause control
codeExamples.code: {/* auto-advances only while !paused */}

codeExamples.label: Carousel that auto-advances with no pause
